title: Rivea adenioides
taxonomy: Rivea adenioides belongs to the Apocynaceae family of flowering plants. It is a species of morning glory, which is a type of flowering plant native to tropical regions. The classification of Rivea adenioides includes the kingdom Plantae, followed by the division Magnoliophyta and then the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification includes the order Gentianales and the family Apocynaceae. At the genus and species level, it is classified as Rivea adenioides.

intro: Rivea adenioides, also known as Half-Shrunk Morning Glory, is a perennial climbing plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic properties, which can help in alleviating pain, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Rivea adenioides include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
